findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is always True regardless of the implementation. This test can never fail and provides zero coverage of Settings behaviour.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion, e.g. assert isinstance(s, Settings), or assert s.environment == 'dev' to verify a real default.

findings.message: Over-mocked test: `resolved_log_level` is patched to return a hardcoded string, then the test asserts that hardcoded string. The real implementation is never exercised. This test will always pass even if `resolved_log_level` is completely broken or deleted.
findings.suggestion: Remove the mock and assert the actual resolution logic, e.g. `assert Settings(environment='dev').resolved_log_level == 'DEBUG'` (already covered by test_resolved_log_level_is_debug_in_dev; delete this test entirely if it adds no new scenario).
